description: Figure 1 presents X-ray diffraction patterns of low-density Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.2C-xCr steel strips, comparing as-cast (a) and annealed (b) conditions for 0Cr, 3Cr, and 6Cr alloys. In panel (a), all as-cast samples display face-centered-cubic austenite (γ, red indices) and body-centered-cubic δ-ferrite (δ, blue indices). A conspicuous double peak between 43.5° and 45.0° corresponds to overlapping (110)δ and (220)DO3 reflections; the area ratio of (110)δ to (220)DO3 decreases with increasing Cr content, indicating that Cr raises δ-ferrite fraction and fosters incipient DO3 ordering. Panel (b) shows that after annealing at 600 °C for 3 h, the double peak evolves into a dominant (220)DO3 single peak, signifying nearly complete transformation of δ-ferrite into the ordered DO3 phase (green indices), an effect enhanced by higher Cr. Austenite reflections persist across all compositions. These patterns demonstrate that Cr addition promotes δ-ferrite stabilization and its subsequent ordering to DO3 upon annealing, while correlating with suppressed nano-κ-carbide precipitation reported in the microstructural analysis.
